zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Jameson於1455年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 邁克爾時，她的父親Lord Jameson of Oremsby22歲，母親Margaret Mathew13歲。她於大約1469年與Richard Green Jr.結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1532年享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: Jameson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Jamison  .

variants.name: Jamerson
meanings: English: variant of   Jameson  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Jimmerson
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of Jameson , based on a pet form of the personal name James.

variants.name: Jamison
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English and Scots Jami(e)son, Jameson, Jem(i)son ‘son of James’ (see James ). Jameson and Jamison are mainly found in northern England, especially in Northumberland and Durham, while in Scotland the more usual form is Jamieson , but all three versions occur on either side of the border.

variants.name: Jimerson
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Jameson  , based on a pet form of the personal name.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Jameson was born in 1455, in Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Lord Jameson of Oremsby, was 22 and her mother, Margaret Mathew, was 13. She married Richard Green Jr. about 1469. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1532, at the age of 77.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Jameson родилась в 1455 г. в местности ормесби ст мачаел, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Lord Jameson of Oremsby было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Mathew, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Green Jr. около 1469 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1532 г. в возрасте 77 лет.

es.Ormesby, Norfolk, England: Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Jameson nació en 1455, en Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Lord Jameson of Oremsby, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Margaret Mathew, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Richard Green Jr. aproximadamente 1469. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1532, a la edad de 77 años.
